title: Cell Lysis Without Detergents
author: Teesha C Luehr
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.y4ffytn

steps.title: Culture HeLa cells in Dulbecco's modified...
steps.instruction: Culture HeLa cells in Dulbecco's modified Eagle's medium (DMEM) containing 10% FBS, 20mM glutamine, and 1% PenStrep.

steps.title: Resuspend the cell pellet(s) in 1
steps.instruction: Resuspend the cell pellet(s) in 1.5 mL, ice cold Milli-Q water Perform the lysis and digestion in the 15 mL, conical tube . The lysis volumes, sonification, and digestion require the larger tube volume.

steps.title: Add 200 µL, 2M tris for...
steps.instruction: Add 200 µL, 2M tris for a final concentration of 100 millimolar (mM)

steps.title: Dilute the sample to a total...
steps.instruction: Dilute the sample to a total of 15 mL, 50mM NaHCO3 This is performed to dilute the TFE.

steps.title: Using a 15 mL, conical tube...
steps.instruction: Using a 15 mL, conical tube pellet cells for 00:05:00, at 300 g Pellet in a tube a minimum size of 15 mL as this tube will be used through till the end of digestion.

steps.title: Wash cells with 10 mL, cold PBS
steps.instruction: Wash cells with 10 mL, cold PBS

steps.title: Pellet cells for 00:05:00, at 300 g
steps.instruction: Pellet cells for 00:05:00, at 300 g

steps.title: Carefully discard supernatant
steps.instruction: Carefully discard supernatant

steps.title: Original protocol uses 5x10 7 cells per sample
steps.instruction: Original protocol uses 5x10 7 cells per sample. Use 2x10 7 cells. Use one 15cm Petri dish or 2-3 10 cm dishes. Each 10cm dish gives 8.8x10 6 cells.

steps.title: Store cell pellet on ice If...
steps.instruction: Store cell pellet on ice If not performing cell lysis immediately, the pellet can be stored at -80 °C, until further use.

steps.title: Ice Bucket
steps.instruction: Ice Bucket

steps.title: 2 Molarity (M), tris at pH 8
steps.instruction: 2 Molarity (M), tris at pH 8.5 Add 1.211 g, tris-base and 243 µL, concentrated HCl (37%) up to a total of 5 mL, in Milli-Q water Calculations done with https://www.cytographica.com/lab/HHTris.html . Tris-base is in NCE 438 chemical room on the top right doubles shelf A stock conical tube of 2 Molarity (M), tris is stored on the bench.

steps.title: 100 millimolar (mM), TCEP Add 250
steps.instruction: 100 millimolar (mM), TCEP Add 250.187 mg, tris(2-carboethyl)phosphine to 10 mL, Milli-Q water TCEP is located in NCE 436 -20C freezer door (currently the top shelf) 405 µL, aliquots of 100 millimolar (mM), TCEP have been made and are stored in Teesha's -20 °C, storage box

steps.title: 400 Molarity (M), CAA Add 374
steps.instruction: 400 Molarity (M), CAA Add 374.04 mg, 2-chloroacetamide to 10 mL, Milli-Q water CAA is located in NCE 438 chemical room on the "C" shelf 405 µL, aliquots of 400 millimolar (mM), CAA have been made and are stored in Teesha's -20 °C, storage box

steps.title: 50 millimolar (mM), NH4HCO3 Add 197
steps.instruction: 50 millimolar (mM), NH4HCO3 Add 197.4 mg, ammonium bicarbonate to 50 mL, Milli-Q water Upscale or downscale volume needed. Up to 15 mL will be used for each replicate.

steps.title: Add 1
steps.instruction: Add 1.5 mL, trifluoroethanol This step should be done in the fume hood. 1:1 water-TFE acts as a hypotonic aqueous buffer to lyse cells, eliminating the need for detergent. TFE helps protein solubility and denaturation; it readily evaporates, so removing it is easy. TFE is located in the NCE 435 flammable cabinet. TFE evaporates fast, so work quickly.

steps.title: Cool for 00:10:00, on ice
steps.instruction: Cool for 00:10:00, on ice

steps.title: Mix the sample for 00:01:00, with a vortex
steps.instruction: Mix the sample for 00:01:00, with a vortex

steps.title: Sonicate the sample for 00:02:00 with...
steps.instruction: Sonicate the sample for 00:02:00 with the Branson Digital Sonifier 250 at 30% amplitude in pulse mode (pulse ON for 0.2s and pulse OFF for 0.8s) with the tapered micro tip probe. The Branson Sonifier is the cell distrupter of choice, however, the bench top water bath sonicator can be used for 10 minutes.

steps.title: Add 400 µL, 100 mM TCEP...
steps.instruction: Add 400 µL, 100 mM TCEP for a final concentration of 10 millimolar (mM)

steps.title: Add 400 µL, 400 mM CAA...
steps.instruction: Add 400 µL, 400 mM CAA for a final concentration of 40 millimolar (mM)

steps.title: Incubate in the heating block for 00:10:00 at 95 °C
steps.instruction: Incubate in the heating block for 00:10:00 at 95 °C

steps.title: Measure the protein concentration with a...
steps.instruction: Measure the protein concentration with a NanoDrop (using SCOPES A205 Protein)

steps.title: Calculate how much LysC is needed...
steps.instruction: Calculate how much LysC is needed for a 1 µg, LysC : 100 µg, protein

steps.title: Add the calculated amount of LysC...
steps.instruction: Add the calculated amount of LysC to the sample LysC is in the -80 °C, freezer

steps.title: Incubate for 02:00:00 at 37 °C, in the digestion incubator
steps.instruction: Incubate for 02:00:00 at 37 °C, in the digestion incubator

steps.title: Calculate how much trypsin is needed...
steps.instruction: Calculate how much trypsin is needed for a 1 µg, trypsin : 100 µg, protein

steps.title: Add the calculated amount of trypsin...
steps.instruction: Add the calculated amount of trypsin to the sample Trypsin is in NCE 435 -20 freezer in the door, bottom shelf

steps.title: Incubate for a minimum of 16:00:00...
steps.instruction: Incubate for a minimum of 16:00:00 at 37 °C, in the digestion incubator

steps.title: Mix the sample for 00:00:05, with a vortex
steps.instruction: Mix the sample for 00:00:05, with a vortex

steps.title: Mix the sample for 00:00:05, with a vortex
steps.instruction: Mix the sample for 00:00:05, with a vortex

steps.title: Mix the sample for 00:00:05, with a vortex
steps.instruction: Mix the sample for 00:00:05, with a vortex

steps.title: 95 °C, heating block
steps.instruction: 95 °C, heating block

steps.title: Calculate how much trypsin is needed...
steps.instruction: Calculate how much trypsin is needed for a 1 µg, trypsin : 100 µg, protein A second trypsin digestion is optional. User digression is advised.

steps.title: Add the trypsin to the sample
steps.instruction: Add the trypsin to the sample

steps.title: Incubate for 05:00:00 at 37 °C, in the digestion incubator
steps.instruction: Incubate for 05:00:00 at 37 °C, in the digestion incubator

steps.title: Proceed to the STAGE tip protocol
steps.instruction: Proceed to the STAGE tip protocol

steps.title: Calculate the total amount of protein...
steps.instruction: Calculate the total amount of protein that is desired to carry forward with the experiment. Keeping in mind that the final peptide concentration will be approximately 10-50% of the protein concentration at this step. Transfer this volume to a new tube. If small enough, transfer to a 2 mL, lo-bind tube .
